goals: Deliver a ComfyUI node with a custom React widget that previews GLSL effects.
Use Three.js ShaderMaterial fed by shaders from the shared JS shader loader (PRD 001).
Output the selected effect name as a string type that the Video Generator Node consumes.
Keep the node self-contained: no server round-trip needed for the preview.

userStories.title: Effect Dropdown
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI user, I want a dropdown in the node that lists all available GLSL effects so that I can select the effect I want to apply without editing configuration files.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The node widget renders a `<select>` (or equivalent R3F/Leva control) listing every
Changing the dropdown selection updates the preview within 100 ms without a page reload.
The selected effect name is emitted as the node's `EFFECT_NAME` output string.

userStories.title: Live GLSL Preview
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI user, I want to see my input image rendered with the selected GLSL shader in the node widget so that I can judge the effect before committing to a full video render.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The widget contains a Three.js canvas (via R3F) showing a plane mesh with the input
The preview animates `u_time` in real time (requestAnimationFrame loop).
`u_resolution` is set to the canvas dimensions and updates on resize.
If no input image is connected, the canvas shows a grey placeholder without errors.

userStories.title: Node Inputs and Outputs
userStories.description: As a ComfyUI workflow builder, I want the Effect Selector Node to accept an IMAGE input and produce IMAGE + EFFECT_NAME outputs so that I can chain it with the Video Generator Node in a standard workflow.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The node is registered in `NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS` as `"CoolEffectSelector"`.
The node's Python class declares input `image` of type `IMAGE` and outputs
The Python `execute` method passes `image` through unchanged and returns the
The node appears in the ComfyUI node browser under the category `"CoolEffects"`.

userStories.title: Shader Loading in Frontend
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want the widget to load GLSL source via the JS shader loader from PRD 001 so that shaders are not duplicated between frontend and backend.

acceptanceCriteria.text: If `loadShader` throws, the widget displays an inline error message inside the canvas

functionalRequirements.description: The Python node class file is `nodes/effect_selector.py`.
The React widget is registered via ComfyUI's frontend extension system under `web/effect_selector.js`.
The widget must use R3F (`@react-three/fiber`) and drei (`@react-three/drei`) for the 3D canvas.
The Three.js `ShaderMaterial` must set `u_image`, `u_time`, and `u_resolution` uniforms on every frame.
The effect dropdown must be populated dynamically from the list of available shader names
The node output type for the effect name must be the ComfyUI built-in `STRING` type.
